state_snapshot.overall_goal: Optimize the Alice Viewer 2 rendering pipeline by implementing topological alignment and vectorized batching while restoring critical UI functionality.
state_snapshot.active_constraints: - **Topological Integrity**: Batch flushes are primitive-aligned (count % 3 == 0 for triangles). - **Vectorized Performance**: drawTriangleArray implements tight chunking to minimize function-call overhead. - **Legacy UI**: Standard camera bookmarks restored in ImGui.
state_snapshot.key_knowledge: - **Alignment Artifacts**: OSM/Quad artifacts were caused by flushing mid-triangle; now resolved via topological checks in add(). - **Chunking Logic**: drawTriangleArray fills the batch up to threshold boundaries, ensuring O(N/K) throughput. - **Switchboard Refactor**: sketch.cpp now uses array-based ingestion, stripping the per-triangle draw loop.
state_snapshot.artifact_trail: - `include/AliceViewer.h`: Declared `drawTriangleArray`. - `src/AliceViewer.cpp`: Implemented topological flush alignment and vectorized `drawTriangleArray`; restored Camera UI. - `src/sketch.cpp`: Refactored `drawMesh` to use `drawTriangleArray`. - `build/bin/AliceViewer.exe`: Linked and optimized stable binary.
state_snapshot.task_state: 1. [DONE] Architectural Healing: OpenNURBS and Reversed-Z. 2. [DONE] Topological Healing: Aligned batch flushing. 3. [DONE] UI Restoration: Bookmark buttons restored. 4. [DONE] Vectorized Performance: Array-based primitive ingestion. 5. [IN PROGRESS] Final Visual QA: Handoff for frame-rate and artifact confirmation.
